title: DeepSeek is designing its own inference chip, sources tell Reuters
subtitle: The chip targets inference rather than training and would loosen the lab's dependence on Nvidia and Huawei silicon.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek is developing an in-house AI chip aimed at inference workloads, according to Reuters reporting confirmed by Bloomberg and picked up broadly this week. The chip is designed to serve responses from existing trained models, not to train new ones, and the work is still early — the company has been reaching out to external partners and holding discussions with chip-design houses, foundries, and memory suppliers. The effort began roughly a year ago.

DeepSeek has, until now, run its globally deployed models on a mix of Nvidia H-series and Huawei Ascend hardware. An in-house inference part would cut the marginal cost per token served, insulate the company from further US export controls, and reduce dependence on Huawei's Ascend roadmap at a moment when Alibaba and Baidu are also pushing their own silicon. The move pairs with reporting from earlier this month that DeepSeek is preparing to raise about $7 billion in a maiden funding round at a $52–$59 billion valuation, ending a yearslong policy of refusing outside capital.

This fits a broader pattern across the Chinese AI stack: labs that were dependent buyers of American accelerators eighteen months ago are now becoming designers, first for inference — where the workload is regular and the software stack is simpler — and eventually for training. Combined with Baidu's Q1 AI Cloud Infra revenue up 79 percent year over year, the picture is one of a domestic AI compute market that is expanding faster than Huawei alone can supply, and that has both the capital and the political cover to build its own alternatives.

For learners: inference chips are where the real economics of deploying AI models live. Understanding the difference between training-optimized silicon (a lot of memory, exotic interconnects, large batches) and inference-optimized silicon (low-latency, high utilization at batch size 1, cheap per token) is one of the highest-leverage mental models you can build if you want to reason about where AI costs actually go.

sources.title: Exclusive-China's DeepSeek developing its own AI chip, sources say — Reuters via US News
